entries.definition:
braided bracelet; key, treasure, safe; neckband, to adorn with a
necklace, to honour, (of animals) to mark with a neckband, to
entrust with a task, to appoint, to undertake a task; cream; share;
to emulate, to follow blindly. إِقْلِيد ʾiqlīd, key, is described by some
philologists as a possible borrowing. Of this root, two forms
occur twice each in the Qur’an: قَلَائِد qalāʾid and مَقَالِيد maqālīd.

قَلَائِد qalāʾid [pl. of n. قِلَادَة qilādatun] necklaces, neckbands,
ornamental neckbands, garlands [put around necks of sacrificial
animals], wreaths (5:2) يَاأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تُحِلُّوا شَعَائِرَ اللَّهِ وَلَا الشَّهْرَ الْحَرَامَ
وَلَا الْهَدْيَ وَلَا الْقَلَائِدَ وَلَا آمِّينَ الْبَيْتَ الْحَرَامَ you who believe, do not violate
the sanctity of God’s rites, the sacred month, the offerings,
[particularly] the garlands, nor those going to the Sacred House.

مَقَالِيد maqālīd [pl. of n. مِقْلَاد miqlād or a pl. with no singular]
key; treasury, case (42:12) لَهُ مَقَالِيدُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ to Him [belong]
the keys of the heavens and the earth.

entries.definition:
q–l–l to be, or become little, small, or few; to trifle with; to lift
up; to accompany; summit of a mountain; to travel around, to be
agile. Of this root, seven forms occur 75 times in the Qur’an: قَلَّ
qalla once; قَلَّل qallala once; أَقَلَّ ʾaqalla once; قَلِيل qalīl 68
times; قَلِيلُون qalīlūn once; قَلِيلَة qalīlatun once and أَقَلّ ʾaqall twice.

قَلَّ qalla i [v. intrans.] to be or become less, to decrease (4:7)
وَلِلنِّسَاءِ نَصِيبٌ مِمَّا تَرَكَ الْوَالِدَانِ وَالْأَقْرَبُونَ مِمَّا قَلَّ مِنْهُ أَوْ كَثُرَ and to the women
is a share of what the parents and kinsmen leave, be it little or
much.

قَلَّل qallala [v. II, trans.] to cause to decrease, to cause to
appear less (8:44) وَيُقَلِّلُكُمْ فِي أَعْيُنِهِمْ and He made you seem few in

774
entries.definition_english:

774
